mechanism_narrative: Persistent near-zero federal funds rate signals a liquidity trap environment where further easing is ineffective, causing investors to price in eventual policy normalization, which pushes 10-year Treasury yields upward over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20-day forward yields is negative in more than 50% of rolling 2-year windows, or average yield change in zero-rate periods is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 7.5% signals accelerating labor market recovery, which strengthens growth expectations and raises the 10-year Treasury yield due to anticipated higher real rates and reduced risk of deflation.
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE crosses below 7.5%, the subsequent 20-day yield change is negative more often than positive (i.e., success rate < 50%).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in industrial production and retail sales indicate robust aggregate demand and supply-side expansion, which drives up the 10-year yield as investors price in stronger future output and potentially higher inflation.
failure_condition: When both INDPRO and RSAFS are above their respective 3-month averages, the 20-day yield change is negative in more than 55% of cases, or the joint signal yields Rank IC < 0.03 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is near zero, an acceleration in CPI signals impending normalization of monetary policy, which raises the 10-year yield as the market anticipates rate hikes. This mechanism is conditional on being in a zero-lower-bound regime.
failure_condition: In ZLB regimes with CPI YoY > 2%, the 20-day yield change is negative more than 50% of the time, or the regime-conditional Rank IC is below 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts are a leading indicator of residential investment and consumer demand. An upward trend in starts signals economic strengthening that raises the 10-year yield through improved growth expectations and potential future housing-related inflation.
failure_condition: The 60-day yield change following a sequence of increasing housing starts (2 consecutive monthly rises) is negative in more than 50% of instances, or the correlation between housing starts and subsequent yield moves is not significantly positive (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate above 0.25% signals the start of a monetary tightening cycle, which pushes the 10-year yield up as markets anticipate higher future short-term rates and tighter financial conditions.
failure_condition: Within 60 trading days following the trigger, the yield change is negative more than 50% of the time, or the mean yield change is not significantly positive at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in nonfarm payrolls and industrial production indicate broad-based economic momentum. This combination of labor market improvement and output growth raises the 10-year yield as investors revise up growth and inflation expectations.
failure_condition: When both PAYEMS and INDPRO month-over-month changes are positive, the 20-day yield change is negative in more than 55% of cases, or the joint signal's Rank IC is below 0.04 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is above its natural rate (~5%), a decline in the unemployment rate strengthens growth expectations and raises yields, as the economy has slack without overheating. This mechanism is conditional on being in a high-unemployment regime, where the direction is positive; the direction would reverse if unemployment were below the natural rate.
failure_condition: In periods with UNRATE > 5%, the correlation between UNRATE decreases and subsequent 20-day yield increases is not significantly positive (p > 0.10), or the regime-conditional Rank IC is below 0.03.
